note: MINING BLOCK fix: replaced the bogus '~869m (theo)' figure with the honest '~10m 00s (target)' value. The old number divided E[n]≈288M expected hashes by a hardcoded 5.5 nonces/sec rate — but the nonce field stored in block headers is the winning thread's nonce, not the total network attempts, so dividing was apples-vs-oranges. Now we display the protocol design target (600s) next to the empirical avg288. Recalculated fall-back also uses the empirical avg288. Tooltip clarified. Hard reload (Ctrl+Shift+R).
